By JEFFREY K. SMITH

The future of energy is in doubt.

The energy crisis has been a long time coming.

The United States has been the world's largest producer and
consumer of energy for more than a century.

But the United States is now the world's largest importer of

dropping.

natural gas.

The United States is also the world's largest consumer of electricity.

Enron's $1.2 Billion Bid for Dynegy Falls Apart
By JEFFREY A. GOLDBERG

11/04/2001
The New York Times
Page 1, Column 5
c. 2001 New York Times Company

NEW YORK -- Enron's bid for Dynegy fell apart on Monday, as the energy trading giant said it would not be able to pay Dynegy's $1.2 billion in debt. 
The company's chief financial officer, Andrew S. Fastow, said that Enron would have to issue more shares to pay off the debt. 
Enron's shares fell $1.50 to $20.50 in trading on the New York Stock Exchange. 
The company's shares have fallen more than 80 percent since the beginning of the year, and the company's debt has risen to $10 billion. 
Enron's shares have fallen so far this year because of concerns about its ability to pay off its debt.
